reforms.description: The Swiss government initiated the Priority Programmes to finance joint industry and academia research projects in strategic areas such as information technologies and biotechnology, aiming to strengthen the links between the private sector and research institutions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Strengthening collaboration between industry and academia enhances innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been actively financing projects since its initiation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in how research is funded and conducted in Switzerland.
reforms.source_quote: The Priority Programmes, initiated in 1992, provide financing for joint industry/academia research projects in areas perceived to be of strategic economic interest.

reforms.description: The Swiss government established Specialised Colleges of Higher Education to enhance professional education and improve technical and management skills in enterprises, emphasizing cooperation with the business sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving education and skills in the workforce supports innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The colleges have been operational and contributing to workforce development since their establishment.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly enhances the education system to better align with industry needs.
reforms.source_quote: This step was followed in the autumn of 1995 by the passage of legislation creating Specialised Colleges of Higher Education.

reforms.description: The Swiss Parliament approved expenditures of SF 220 million for the promotion of scientific research in the framework of national and European programmes, alongside a commitment of about SF 3.9 billion during 1996-99 for the promotion of universities and research.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for research enhances knowledge creation and innovation capacity.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding has been allocated and utilized for research projects since approval.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a major commitment to funding scientific research and higher education.
reforms.source_quote: The Parliament approves of government decisions on the promotion of universities and research, for which the Confederation will provide about SF 3.9 billion during 1996-99.
